metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: gap:interrupt
=> frozen: kiro emits NO interrupt marker in the tailed .jsonl (cancellation lands only in the <uuid>.json sidecar's end_reason: Cancelled, which the parser never reads), so the post-ESC working→ready settle is unobservable.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable; driver_capability: gap:interrupt
=> route: frozen (daemon-incapable outranks the driver gap — the more fundamental observability problem wins; computeRoute checks `daemon == "incapable"` before the `gap:` prefix, router.go:43-48).

## Scenario

user-esc-interrupt asserts the user-observable arc `ready → working → ready`: the user sends a prompt that takes >2s, state flips to `working`, the user presses ESC to cancel the in-flight turn BEFORE the agent emits a turn-done marker, and state must settle back to `ready` (must NOT stick in `working`). A follow-up prompt then drives a fresh `ready → working → ready` arc proving the parser reset. The load-bearing assertion is the post-interrupt `working → ready` settle.

## agent_supports: yes

kiro-cli 2.5.1 records turn cancellation. A sibling binary scan found the turn-end reason enum includes `Cancelled` alongside `UserTurnEnd`, and the live smoke confirms `end_reason` is a real per-turn field at `session_state.conversation_metadata.user_turn_metadatas[].end_reason` in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar (the smoke session captured `UserTurnEnd` for its two clean turns; FINDINGS.md §4). The TUI binds ESC/Ctrl-C to cancel an in-flight turn (interactive REPL with a per-turn completion footer `▸ Credits: … • Time: …s`). So the agent unambiguously supports user ESC cancellation, and it persists the fact in the sidecar.

## daemon_capability: incapable

The daemon tails the `.jsonl` Source (`agent.FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ".kiro/sessions/cli", JSONLineParser}`, agent.go) — NOT the `<uuid>.json` sidecar (the adapter reads the sidecar only for cwd, via `transcript.ExtractCWDFromSidecar` in pid.go). The interrupt signal is invisible to that tailed Source for three compounding reasons:

1. **The `.jsonl` event vocabulary carries no cancellation kind.** The only kinds kiro writes to the transcript are `Prompt`, `AssistantMessage`, `ToolResults`, `Clear` (FINDINGS.md §4, confirmed against the live smoke `.jsonl`). There is no `turn_aborted`/`Cancelled` line in the `.jsonl` — the cancellation lands ONLY in the sidecar's `user_turn_metadatas[].end_reason`. The parser only reads the `.jsonl`.

2. **The kiro parser sets NO `IsUserInterrupt`.** `grep -ni interrupt|abort|cancel core/adapters/inbound/agents/kirocli/*.go` returns nothing. parser.go maps `Prompt→user_message`, `AssistantMessage(toolUse)→assistant`, `AssistantMessage(text-only)→turn_done`, `ToolResults→tool_result`, `Clear→skip` — and never emits the `IsUserInterrupt` flag that classifier rule 3 keys on. So rule 3 (state_classifier.go:86-95: `working/waiting + LastEventType=="user" + LastWasUserInterrupt + !HasOpenToolCall → ready`) can NEVER fire for kiro-cli, because `LastWasUserInterrupt` is promoted from `parsed.IsUserInterrupt` (tailer.go:904) which kiro never sets.

3. **No turn_done fires on the truncated turn.** After an ESC mid-turn, the `.jsonl` is a truncated turn whose last line is an `AssistantMessage` carrying a `toolUse` block (mid-turn → `assistant`, keeps the session `working`) or a `ToolResults` — with NO trailing text-only `AssistantMessage`. Since the turn-end marker is precisely "a text-only AssistantMessage" (parser.go:76-79), no `turn_done` is emitted, so classifier rule 2 (`IsAgentDone()`) never fires either. The session was driven to `working` by the `Prompt` and STAYS `working`.

With neither rule 2 nor rule 3 able to fire, there is no `working → ready` settle. There is also no idle-sweep fallback: the PID-liveness sweep only TTL-cleans `ready` sessions or reaps PID-dead ones — a `working` session with a live REPL PID is never settled to `ready`. Recording this cell today would capture a stuck-`working` shape (until the follow-up prompt or `process_exited`), which proves the OPPOSITE of the scenario's intent. This is the codex/user-esc-interrupt precedent exactly: agent=yes, daemon=incapable, frozen.

## daemon: incapable vs bug (the line)

This is `incapable`, not `bug`, under the SKILL's definition: `bug` requires a trace in the tailed Source that the daemon mis-handles; `incapable` is when no trace exists in that Source. The cancellation trace exists ONLY in the sidecar, which is NOT the tailed Source — from the `.jsonl` the daemon tails for state, no interrupt signal exists, so the 3-state model cannot represent the settle. (It is materially weaker than codex, where the unparsed `turn_aborted` at least lived in the SAME transcript the daemon tails — there the fix is a pure parser addition. For kiro the fix would require wiring a NEW Source: a sidecar end_reason reader feeding the state pipeline.)

## driver_capability: gap:interrupt

The recipe needs an `interrupt` step (Escape/Ctrl-C to the tmux pane mid-turn). The kiro interactive driver stubs it: `interrupt) not_implemented interrupt` (driver-interactive.sh:245), and `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"` (line 86) does NOT list `interrupt`, so recipe-lint flags it as a semantic_gap. The seam must be ported from drive-claudecode/codex-interactive.sh (`tmux send-keys Escape`). This is tooling work, NOT the observability limit — it does not change the route (frozen wins), but it is the honest driver verdict.

## Why frozen (route), and what would unblock it

The load-bearing user-observable assertion — state returns to `ready` after the ESC interrupt, not stuck in `working` — cannot be observed by the daemon as it exists today. Per the pipeline rule the cell is frozen: no recording. Two things must land to unfreeze it: (a) DAEMON — teach the kiro adapter to read `<uuid>.json`'s `user_turn_metadatas[].end_reason == "Cancelled"` and emit a turn-end/interrupt signal feeding the classifier (a new sidecar Source/MetricsReader, already noted as a future enhancement in the adapter decisions), so the working→ready settle fires; and (b) DRIVER — port the `interrupt` step. Re-assess to yes/full/ready and proceed to record once both land.

## Confidence 0.78

The parser/classifier read is high-confidence and code-grounded (the kiro parser provably sets no IsUserInterrupt; rule 3 provably cannot fire). The residual uncertainty is the EXACT post-ESC `.jsonl` shape: the live smoke never performed an interrupt, so whether the truncated turn ends on an `assistant`(toolUse) line vs a `ToolResults` line (both leave the session `working`) is inferred, not observed. Either way the settle is unobservable, so the incapable verdict holds; a live recording in a future record pass is the only thing that can promote this from provisional to settled (backflow loop).
caveats: BACKFLOW (provisional): the exact post-ESC .jsonl shape was NOT observed live — the 2.5.1 smoke captured only clean turns (end_reason UserTurnEnd). The incapable verdict is inferred from the .jsonl event vocabulary (no Cancelled kind) + the parser setting no IsUserInterrupt; a future live interrupt recording is the only thing that can settle whether the truncated turn leaves any .jsonl-observable trace the daemon could settle on. If it does, record's backflow loop should correct the pillar via of cell write in the same commit.

The cancellation IS persisted by kiro — but only in the <uuid>.json sidecar (session_state.conversation_metadata.user_turn_metadatas[].end_reason == Cancelled), which the adapter reads for cwd only (ExtractCWDFromSidecar) and never for state. Unfreezing the daemon pillar is a sidecar end_reason reader, not a .jsonl parser tweak — materially more work than the codex turn_aborted fix.

driver:gap:interrupt does not change the route (frozen, because daemon-incapable outranks a driver gap per computeRoute). It is recorded honestly so that if the daemon pillar is later unfrozen, the driver gap is already flagged for record to port the interrupt seam.

sources.note: Sets NO IsUserInterrupt anywhere; event kinds handled are Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear only. Turn-end = text-only AssistantMessage (lines 76-79). After an ESC the truncated turn has no trailing text-only AssistantMessage → no turn_done.

Rule 3 (lines 86-95) requires LastWasUserInterrupt (+ LastEventType==user + !HasOpenToolCall) to drive working→ready on ESC; rule 2 requires IsAgentDone(). kiro emits neither signal, so no working→ready settle fires — the session sticks in working.

LastWasUserInterrupt is promoted from parsed.IsUserInterrupt (line ~904). The kiro parser never sets IsUserInterrupt, so this sticky flag is always false for kiro-cli — classifier rule 3 is unreachable.

Source is FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ".kiro/sessions/cli", JSONLineParser} — the daemon tails the .jsonl, NOT the <uuid>.json sidecar. pid.go reads the sidecar only for cwd (ExtractCWDFromSidecar). The cancellation end_reason lives only in the untailed sidecar.

§4: .jsonl event vocabulary is Prompt/AssistantMessage/ToolResults/Clear — no model/usage/cost and no cancellation kind; sidecar <uuid>.json holds per-turn metadata. §5: turn-end is the final text-only AssistantMessage. The smoke recorded NO interrupt (end_reason captured = UserTurnEnd only), so the post-ESC .jsonl shape is unobserved.

interrupt) not_implemented interrupt (line 245, stubbed); DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep" (line 86) omits interrupt → recipe-lint semantic_gap. The interrupt seam (tmux send-keys Escape) must be ported from drive-claudecode/codex-interactive.sh → driver_capability: gap:interrupt.

Calibration precedent (codex's interrupted-turn cell, scenario_id user-esc-interrupt): agent=yes, daemon=incapable, frozen for the same root cause (the turn_aborted cancellation marker is unparsed; no working→ready settle observable). kiro is the same shape, with the wrinkle that kiro's Cancelled marker is in a separate sidecar, not the tailed transcript — a strictly weaker daemon position than codex.

computeRoute (lines 39-51): daemon=="incapable" returns RouteFrozen BEFORE the driver gap:* check — so the route is frozen regardless of driver_capability=gap:interrupt. The daemon (observability) problem outranks the tooling (driver) gap.

preconditions: FROZEN — not recordable today. The daemon cannot observe the post-ESC working→ready settle (kiro emits no interrupt marker in the tailed .jsonl; the Cancelled end_reason lives only in the untailed <uuid>.json sidecar). Recording would capture a stuck-working shape that contradicts the scenario.

UNFREEZE (daemon): teach the kiro adapter to read <uuid>.json user_turn_metadatas[].end_reason==Cancelled and feed a turn-end/interrupt signal to the classifier (sidecar end_reason reader / MetricsReader — already a documented future enhancement).
UNFREEZE (driver): port the interrupt step (tmux send-keys Escape) into replaydata/agents/kiro-cli/driver-interactive.sh and add `interrupt` to DRIVE_ELICITS.

script.text: Write a long detailed essay about the history of computing, at least 500 words. Take your time and stream the response.
Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: FROZEN: no recording. Once unfrozen, the spec to verify is ready → working → ready: the post-ESC interrupt must settle the session back to ready (not stick in working), and the follow-up prompt drives a clean ready → working → ready second turn.
